evidence: `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` keeps a deprecated `supabase` proxy with lazy runtime fallback initialization, so import sites can bypass explicit bootstrap order.
`src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts` exposes `setErrorPresenter` and mutates notifier behavior globally, creating cross-module hidden state coupling.
`src/shared/hooks/useMobile.tsx` and `src/types/generationAndShots.ts` are compatibility re-export surfaces that keep legacy import paths alive instead of enforcing domain boundaries.

`src/features/gallery/hooks/useAdjacentPagePreloader.ts` ties its filter type to another hook’s positional parameter (`Parameters<typeof fetchGenerations>[3]`), making ownership and contracts implicit.

`src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem/components/ShotActions.tsx` relies on four prop-bag interfaces (`selector`, `status`, `quickCreate`, `actions`) plus many callbacks, indicating orchestration spread across modules rather than a cohesive boundary.

`src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts` and `src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem/hooks/useMediaGalleryItemState.ts` split related media-item flow (navigation, modal transitions, error handling, shot creation) across multiple surfaces with overlapping responsibilities.

src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useButtonGroupProps.ts is a pure pass-through repack of three prop groups with no validation/translation, then those same shapes are repacked again in src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx and src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx.

src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts builds a very large ImageEditValue param bag by stitching many sub-hooks, and src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useImageEditValue.ts then reconstructs the same fields into another object layer.
useImageEditValue memoizes with dependency [params], but useInlineEditState creates a new params object each render, so memoization rarely provides leverage while preserving high indirection cost.

travel payload shaping is split across src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts and src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts with duplicated legacy-structure-parameter handling and independent normalization paths, increasing seam drift risk.

Server completion routing in supabase/functions/complete_task/generation.ts and supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts relies on broad params probing in many alternative paths (orchestrator_details, top-level, fallbacks), making boundary contracts implicit instead of explicit.
Client and server handoffs around orchestrator metadata are resilient but ad hoc, requiring multiple fallback checks rather than a single canonical envelope.

src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useShotImagesEditorCallbacks.ts contains dense imperative deletion flow (multi-query read, sibling matching, delete, cache mutation, broad invalidation) in one callback, reducing local clarity and testability.
src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts has very large internal assembly functions and repeated field forwarding that makes local intent hard to scan and maintain.
Despite complexity, helper extraction is present and naming is mostly descriptive, preventing a lower score.
low_level_elegance.issues_preventing_higher_score: Large hook internals still combine state wiring, data access, and side-effect orchestration in single flows; targeted command/query extraction would improve directness and reduce branch-heavy handlers.

evidence: The generation-task API has both status-rich and status-lossy entry points: `getPrimaryTaskMappingsForGenerations` returns `GenerationTaskMapping.status`, while `getPrimaryTaskIdsForGenerations` drops status/error context and returns only `Map<string, string | null>` (src/shared/lib/generationTaskRepository.ts:123-200).

Public edge/shared APIs keep parallel legacy and modern response shapes: `parseJsonBodyStrict` returns `OperationResult`, while `parseJsonBodyStrictResponse` returns `{ body?, errorResponse? }` (supabase/functions/_shared/edgeRequest.ts:195-223); similarly generate-video request building supports both nested v2 params and legacy flat fields in one public input type (src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/services/generateVideoService.ts:458-526).

The same generate-video module publicly exports many internal/test helper APIs despite comment guidance that consumers should use `generateVideo()` (src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/services/generateVideoService.ts:620-639), which broadens the effective contract surface.

Several files are pure compatibility pass-throughs with long migration/deprecation headers and no behavior (`src/features/prompt-editor/components/BulkEditControls.tsx`, `src/shared/components/BatchDropZone.tsx`, `src/shared/components/ConfirmDialog.tsx`, `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/utils/download.ts`, `src/shared/hooks/useBatchImageLoading.ts`, `src/shared/hooks/useClickRipple.ts`, `src/shared/hooks/useEditVideoSettings.ts`, `src/shared/hooks/useGalleryPageState.ts`, `src/shared/hooks/useMobile.tsx`, `src/shared/lib/NetworkStatusManager.ts`, `src/shared/lib/aspectRatios.ts`, `src/shared/lib/autoplayMonitor.ts`, `src/shared/lib/clientThumbnailGenerator.ts`, `src/shared/lib/deepEqual.ts`, `src/shared/components/ui/cn.ts`).

Comment density is high and often restates implementation details rather than intent (`src/shared/components/ShotImageManager/utils/image-utils.ts`, `src/shared/hooks/useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm.tsx`, `src/shared/hooks/useTextCase.ts`).
The codebase slice shows ceremony-heavy compatibility surfaces likely accumulated from generated/templated migration patterns rather than consolidated cleanup.
Multiple active deprecated shims remain in production paths across components, hooks, and libs, indicating prolonged dual-path imports rather than completed ownership moves.

Sunset markers exist but are not enforced by code-level guards/tests (`@removalTarget 2026-06-30` in `src/shared/components/ui/cn.ts`, `src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts`, `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts`).
Legacy API contracts are still exported for travel-between-images payloads with explicit deprecated fields (`src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/legacyStructureVideo.ts`).
Supabase legacy proxy export (`supabase`) remains available with dynamic proxy behavior in `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts`, preserving old callsites instead of forcing migration.

`src/shared/hooks/` has 95 production files (threshold signal: >10 mixed concerns), and the assigned files in that folder span unrelated responsibilities: `useApiTokens.ts` (account/API-token lifecycle), `useAsyncOperation.ts` (generic async state helper), and `useAutoSaveSettings.ts` (settings persistence/state machine).

The structure coupling shows heavy cross-directory edges from this single folder (`src/shared/hooks/ -> src/integrations/supabase/` = 47, `-> src/shared/lib/` = 57, `-> src/shared/lib/errorHandling/` = 41), indicating the directory acts as a broad catch-all rather than a focused package boundary.

`src/shared/components/` also exceeds flat-folder limits with 80 files and mixed concerns; assigned files include domain UI (`ActiveLoRAsDisplay.tsx`) and global infra tests (`AppErrorBoundary.test.tsx`) in the same top-level component directory, while a dedicated `src/shared/components/__tests__/` area exists.

Media lightbox flow uses very wide contracts: `MediaLightboxProps` defines 63 fields in `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/MediaLightbox.tsx`, then data is re-bundled and forwarded into image/video-specific layers.

`useSharedLightboxState` is a 700+ line aggregator in `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useSharedLightboxState.ts` combining variants, navigation, references, shot operations, lineage, layout, and button models into one hook.

Timeline orchestration remains monolithic: `useTimelineOrchestrator` in `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/Timeline/hooks/useTimelineOrchestrator.ts` manages drag/drop, zoom, selection, endpoint drag, video browser integration, and returns a very large surface consumed by timeline UI.

Shot editor boundaries are still wide-bag APIs: `UseGenerationControllerParams` has 57 fields and `ShotEditorLayoutProps` has 64 fields, indicating responsibility partitioning by file but not by cohesive domain contract.

Task parameter extraction logic is duplicated across components: `TaskDetailsModal` manually checks `input_images`, `full_orchestrator_payload.input_image_paths_resolved`, and `input_image_paths_resolved`, while `useGenerationDetails` separately parses params and derives images.

UI primitives use inconsistent `cn` import conventions: `src/shared/components/ui/button.tsx` imports `cn` from `@/shared/lib/utils` while `src/shared/components/ui/badge.tsx`, `src/shared/components/ui/label.tsx`, and `src/shared/components/ui/toast.tsx` import from `@/shared/components/ui/cn`.

The same UI primitive cluster mixes style conventions (double-quote/no-semicolon style in `src/shared/components/ui/button.tsx` and siblings versus semicolon/single-quote style common in non-UI files like `src/shared/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts`).
convention_outlier.issues_preventing_higher_score: Sibling UI files still expose style and utility-import drift, making the UI layer less predictable to modify.

evidence: `src/features/account/hooks/useApiTokens.ts:42-45` and `:60-63` use `handleAndRethrow`, preserving centralized error strategy.
`src/features/billing/hooks/useCredits.ts:63`, `:86`, `:98` wraps Supabase errors with new generic `Error(...)` strings, dropping structured upstream fields (code/details/hint).
`src/app/bootstrap.tsx:71` swallows dynamic import failure with `.catch(() => undefined)`, unlike neighboring paths that report via `handleError`/`reportRuntimeError`.
error_consistency.issues_preventing_higher_score: Mixed throw/wrap/swallow patterns create inconsistent failure contracts and observability across similar hooks.

evidence: `supabase/functions/complete_task/index.ts` performs explicit auth and ownership enforcement (`authenticateRequest(... allowJwtUserAuth: true)`, service-role branching, and `verifyTaskOwnership` for user tokens).
`supabase/functions/calculate-task-cost/index.ts` consistently enforces service-role-only access via `authenticateRequest` plus `auth.isServiceRole` check before processing.
authorization_consistency.issues_preventing_higher_score: Client routing (`src/app/routes.tsx`) uses a local-storage token presence heuristic for redirect decisions before authoritative auth resolution, which is intentionally documented but still a separate gate style from server-side token/ownership checks.

findings.summary: Multiple compatibility facades preserve legacy paths and concentrate coupling in shared hubs.
evidence: `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` exports deprecated `supabase` proxy and fallback lazy init path instead of hard bootstrap-only access.
`src/shared/hooks/useMobile.tsx` is explicitly marked deprecated but still acts as active compatibility entrypoint for mobile APIs.
`src/types/generationAndShots.ts` is a canonical barrel re-export, keeping broad cross-module dependence on a central alias surface.
findings.suggestion: Run an explicit migration to direct module imports, then remove these compatibility facades behind a dated cleanup milestone; enforce with lint/import rules banning the deprecated entrypoints.

findings.summary: Global mutable error/reporting and lazy runtime recovery create hidden startup-order dependencies.
evidence: `handleError.ts` exposes `setErrorPresenter` which globally rewires notifier behavior via `configureErrorNotifier`.
`getSupabaseClient()` catches runtime access failures and mutates module-level warning state (`hasWarnedFallbackInitialization`) before retrying initialization.
Feature-level hooks such as `useMediaGalleryItemState.ts` call shared `handleError`, tying domain UI flow to a global runtime error singleton.
findings.suggestion: Move error presentation and runtime client access behind explicit app-level providers/context and make hooks consume injected dependencies instead of global mutable module state.

findings.summary: Gallery preloading depends on implicit contracts instead of stable domain-owned interfaces.
evidence: `useAdjacentPagePreloader.ts` derives `GenerationFilters` from `Parameters<typeof fetchGenerations>[3]`, so a positional signature change silently breaks preloader typing/contracts.
The same hook passes both raw `filters` and serialized `filtersString`, indicating coordination logic distributed across call boundaries instead of one canonical query-key/filter contract.
`useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts` and `MediaTypeFilter.tsx` operate on filter state through literal values and callbacks, but no explicit shared filter domain contract is owned at one boundary.
findings.suggestion: Define a dedicated gallery filter/preload contract module (explicit `GenerationFilters` type + key builder + normalizer) and have preloader, handlers, and UI components depend on that stable interface.

findings.summary: Media gallery item behavior is fragmented across prop-bag orchestration surfaces.
evidence: `ShotActions.tsx` requires large grouped props (`selector`, `status`, `quickCreate`, `actions`) and embeds navigation/add business branching in UI component code.
`useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts` duplicates related action concerns (navigate, notifier jump, modal transitions) that are part of the same gallery-item interaction lifecycle.
`useMediaGalleryItemState.ts` owns additional workflow state and async create-shot error flow, further splitting one interaction domain across multiple modules.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a single `useMediaGalleryItemController` (or equivalent) that owns shot-action workflow and exposes a smaller view model to presentational components.

findings.summary: Button-group abstractions add multiple forwarding layers without adding behavior or safety.
evidence: useButtonGroupProps returns a memoized object that mirrors input fields 1:1.
Video and image render-model assembly then re-wrap topRight/bottomLeft/bottomRight again before passing to LightboxLayout.
No translation, narrowing, validation, or policy is enforced at these layers.
findings.suggestion: Collapse to a single canonical `LightboxButtonGroupProps` producer near shared state and pass it through directly; keep only wrappers that enforce constraints (e.g., readOnly gating, required handlers).

findings.summary: Inline edit state uses a large parameter-bag abstraction with limited payoff and ineffective memo boundaries.
evidence: useInlineEditState composes many sub-hooks into a large object via `buildImageEditValueParams` and passes it to `useImageEditValue`.
useImageEditValue re-splits and re-merges the same fields across `buildModeState`, `buildCanvasAndAnnotationState`, `buildRepositionAndPanelState`, and `buildFormAndGenerationState`.
Memo dependency is `[params]`, but callsite constructs a fresh object every render, so memoization does not effectively stabilize the result.
findings.suggestion: Replace monolithic param bag with a small number of stable slices (mode/canvas/forms/status), memoize those slices at the source, and let `ImageEditProvider` consume slice objects directly.

findings.summary: Travel-task payload normalization is duplicated across paths, creating seam drift risk.
evidence: Both payload builders handle legacy structure-guidance fields and cleanup logic independently.
Individual segment logic keeps a local legacy param list while batch payload builder maintains a separate deletion list.
Each entry point applies defaults/fallbacks differently, increasing divergence across batch vs single-segment generation.
findings.suggestion: Centralize a shared `normalizeTravelParams` module that owns legacy-field translation, structure guidance normalization, and canonical defaults, then consume it from both batch and individual segment builders.

findings.summary: Complete-task routing relies on implicit param probing instead of explicit typed contracts across orchestrator/generation flows.
evidence: generation.ts accepts `taskData: unknown` and branches on many optional nested params (`params`, `orchestrator_details`, fallbacks).
orchestrator.ts computes expected counts and IDs from multiple alternative param locations, implying schema ambiguity at seam boundaries.
calculate-task-cost defines a typed shape, but task param interpretation still depends on loose dictionary access.
findings.suggestion: Introduce shared task param discriminated unions for orchestration/segment/final-step tasks and validate once at function boundary before routing.

findings.summary: Shot image editor callback hook contains a dense, multi-responsibility delete path with intertwined DB and cache concerns.
evidence: handleDeleteSegment performs read-before-delete, sibling discovery, deletion, cache mutation, and broad invalidation in one callback.
The callback also repeats pair-id extraction logic from nested params for both parent and sibling items.
Hook-level data orchestration style matches broader pattern where UI hooks directly manage repository and cache internals.
findings.suggestion: Extract segment deletion into a dedicated service/repository function returning a normalized deletion plan (`idsToDelete`, affectedParents), then keep the hook focused on invoking and reconciling cache updates.

findings.summary: Async failures bypass intended error normalization in tool settings fetch path
evidence: `fetchToolSettingsSupabase` constructs `const promise = (async () => ...)()` and returns it directly from inside `try` (src/shared/lib/toolSettingsService.ts:221-245).

The outer `catch` in that function contains network/auth normalization logic (src/shared/lib/toolSettingsService.ts:246-269), but it will only catch synchronous errors before return, not Promise rejections inside the returned async pipeline.

Contrast: `generateVideo` consistently catches awaited async boundaries and maps them through `failureResult` (src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/services/generateVideoService.ts:677-707, 752-766), showing the intended seam behavior.
findings.suggestion: Await the internal promise inside `fetchToolSettingsSupabase` (`return await promise`) or move normalization into `promise.catch(...)` before returning, so all async failures follow one documented error contract.

findings.summary: Convenience task-ID API drops failure status from the richer mapping contract
evidence: `getPrimaryTaskMappingsForGenerations` returns per-generation `status` values including `query_failed`, `scope_mismatch`, and `invalid_tasks_shape` (src/shared/lib/generationTaskRepository.ts:123-186).
`getPrimaryTaskIdsForGenerations` converts those results to `Map<string, string | null>` and discards status/error information entirely (src/shared/lib/generationTaskRepository.ts:188-200).
Downstream cache logic relies on status to decide logging/prefetch behavior (src/shared/lib/generationTaskCache.ts:49-67), so callers using the lossy API cannot apply equivalent handling safely.
findings.suggestion: Deprecate `getPrimaryTaskIdsForGenerations` or change it to return `{ taskId, status }` values; keep a tiny adapter for legacy call sites that explicitly opt into lossy behavior.

findings.summary: Public API surfaces expose mixed legacy/new request/result shapes
evidence: `TravelRequestBodyInput` accepts both nested V2 groups and many legacy flat fields in one type (src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/services/generateVideoService.ts:470-490), then normalizes at runtime (492-526).
The same module exports multiple internal builders including underscored test helpers and aliases while documenting `generateVideo()` as the public API (620-639).

`edgeRequest` similarly keeps dual parser contracts: `OperationResult`-based (`parseJsonBodyStrict`) and legacy `{ body?, errorResponse? }` adapter (`parseJsonBodyStrictResponse`) (supabase/functions/_shared/edgeRequest.ts:195-223).
findings.suggestion: Define one canonical contract per module (V2 input/result only), move legacy adapters behind explicitly named compatibility modules, and stop exporting internal builder helpers from primary service entrypoints.

findings.summary: Boundary handoffs mix side-effect styles, creating non-uniform orchestration seams
evidence: Invalidation APIs with similar naming have different lifecycle contracts: some only enqueue invalidation (`invalidateGenerationsSync`), while `invalidateVariantChange` additionally awaits a refetch (src/shared/hooks/invalidation/useGenerationInvalidation.ts:164-199).

Preload coordinator mutates shared preloading state (`preloadingService.clearQueue()`) before scheduling and again on cleanup (src/features/gallery/lib/adjacentPagePreloadCoordinator.ts:102-128), coupling one caller's lifecycle to global queue state.
Both modules operate at cache-coordination seams, but expose mixed 'fire-and-forget' vs 'awaited + global mutable queue' behavior that is hard to compose predictably.
findings.suggestion: Standardize seam contracts: split APIs into explicit `schedule*` (non-awaiting) and `run*` (awaiting) variants, and scope preload queue state by request/session key instead of unconditional global clear.

findings.summary: Deprecated compatibility shims are still widely retained, showing migration not actually converged.
evidence: These files export from new locations with only deprecation headers and no runtime behavior, preserving old import paths indefinitely.
The same migration pattern appears across multiple layers (feature components, shared components, hooks, libs), indicating systemic coexistence of old/new APIs.
findings.suggestion: Create a time-boxed shim burn-down: add lint/import-ban rules for old paths, codemod callsites to canonical modules, and remove shim files in one coordinated pass per domain.

findings.summary: The deprecated `supabase` proxy keeps legacy usage alive and masks incomplete runtime-migration adoption.
evidence: `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` still exports `supabase` via `createSupabaseCompatibilityProxy()` despite deprecation/removal target metadata.
Proxy traps dynamically resolve `getSupabaseClient()` on property access, allowing old consumers to continue unchanged instead of failing fast during migration.
Fallback error handling path (`handleError`) further cushions incorrect initialization order, reducing pressure to complete bootstrap migration.
findings.suggestion: Gate legacy proxy behind a strict dev-only flag immediately, add telemetry for remaining imports, then remove `supabase` export and require `initializeSupabase()` + `getSupabaseClient()` usage before removal date.

findings.summary: This slice contains high-ceremony wrappers and restating comments that add maintenance noise without behavior.
evidence: `image-utils.ts` has extensive explanatory comments for straightforward delegation and loops, while functions mostly forward to existing utilities.
`useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm.tsx` includes long narrative usage documentation for a small hook whose behavior is self-evident from function names and JSX props.
Multiple one-line re-export shims are paired with verbose migration headers, amplifying boilerplate footprint.
findings.suggestion: Prune comments to intent/constraints only, collapse trivial wrapper files during migration cleanup, and enforce a guideline that wrappers must add validation/translation/side effects or be removed.

findings.summary: Supabase client access path is overly indirect, with nested recovery branches and proxy traps obscuring straightforward runtime behavior.
evidence: `getSupabaseClient()` has primary try path, recovery logging branch, dev warning branch, and fallback-init try/catch rethrow branch.
`createSupabaseCompatibilityProxy()` introduces dynamic reflective traps that make ordinary property reads/writes perform hidden runtime resolution.
In `useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm.tsx`, component-returning hook closure plus casted `mutation.variables as string` further reflects local indirection over direct state modeling.
findings.suggestion: Replace proxy-based legacy access with explicit function-only API, simplify `getSupabaseClient()` to one recovery policy, and tighten hook return typing by storing typed `deletingId` state directly instead of casted mutation internals.

findings.summary: 95-file shared hooks folder mixes infra, settings, and account-domain logic
evidence: `src/shared/hooks/` has 95 production files (from `holistic_context.structure.directory_profiles`), far above the >10 mixed-concern threshold.
`useApiTokens.ts` manages PAT-specific auth/query/mutation flows; `useAsyncOperation.ts` is generic infra; `useAutoSaveSettings.ts` is a complex persistence/state-machine hook.
A feature path for API-token hooks already exists in repo structure (`src/features/account/hooks/useApiTokens.ts`), indicating current root shared placement has weak affinity.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) create target folders `src/shared/hooks/async/`, `src/shared/hooks/settings/`, and keep domain token logic under `src/features/account/hooks/`; (2) move in order: `useAsyncOperation.ts` (+test) to `shared/hooks/async`, then `useAutoSaveSettings.ts` (+test) to `shared/hooks/settings`, then converge `useApiTokens` to `features/account/hooks` and remove/shared-reexport old path temporarily; (3) update imports via codemod (`rg -l "@/shared/hooks/useAsyncOperation|@/shared/hooks/useAutoSaveSettings|@/shared/hooks/useApiTokens" src | xargs sed ...`) or `desloppify move <src> <dst> --dry-run` then apply; (4) validate with `pnpm vitest src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useAsyncOperation.test.ts src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useAutoSaveSettings.test.ts src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useAutoTopup.test.ts` and `pnpm tsc --noEmit`.

findings.summary: Top-level 'shared' packages hold feature-specific behavior, blurring architectural ownership
evidence: `useApiTokens.ts` contains account-domain token lifecycle operations and auth boundary checks, but sits in a global shared bucket.
`useAutoSaveSettings.ts` combines framework-level concerns with product-level persistence policy in a generic shared location.
`ActiveLoRAsDisplay.tsx` is LoRA-domain UI logic in a broad `shared/components` root that also contains generic and infrastructure components/tests.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) define ownership packages: `src/features/account/hooks/` for API-token behavior, `src/shared/hooks/async/` + `src/shared/hooks/settings/` for reusable infra, and `src/features/lora/components/` (or tool-specific component package) for LoRA UI; (2) move domain components/hooks first (`ActiveLoRAsDisplay.tsx`, `useApiTokens.ts`), then infra hooks (`useAsyncOperation.ts`, `useAutoSaveSettings.ts`) into focused shared subpackages; (3) keep temporary barrel re-exports to avoid big-bang breakage, then remove after callsites migrate; (4) run import and boundary validation with `pnpm tsc --noEmit`, targeted vitest for moved tests, and a dependency check (`rg "@/shared/(hooks|components)/" src/features src/tools`).

findings.summary: Supabase boundary keeps both explicit-runtime and legacy-proxy access paths active.
evidence: README states explicit runtime contract (`initializeSupabase()`/`getSupabaseClient()`), but also keeps legacy import support (`supabase`) during migration.
client.ts exports `supabase` via a proxy (lines 55-99) and `getSupabaseClient()` silently recovers by lazy initialization (lines 25-51), so call sites can bypass explicit bootstrap discipline.
findings.suggestion: Collapse to one boundary in stages: (1) add CI check to block new `import { supabase }` usage, (2) switch fallback recovery to fail-fast in production while keeping dev warning, (3) migrate remaining call sites to `getSupabaseClient()`, and (4) remove proxy export at the documented sunset date.

findings.summary: Contract governance is fragmented across README, deprecation comments, and implicit conventions.
evidence: Deprecation ownership/removal targets appear as local annotations in multiple modules (`client.ts`, `handleError.ts`) without a single runtime-checked registry.
README provides strong removal-gate guidance for type shim migration but no equivalent explicit command gate for Supabase legacy proxy removal, despite a dated removal target in code.
Canonical type-surface messaging (`generationAndShots.ts`) and compatibility policy are spread over files, increasing docs-vs-runtime drift risk.
findings.suggestion: Create one governance contract source (for example `docs/compatibility-contracts.md` + machine-checked script) listing each shim, owner, removal date, and enforcement command; wire all listed gates into `quality:check` so README statements and runtime behavior are validated from the same source.

findings.summary: `src/shared/lib` is an overloaded flat hub, and UI primitives depend on its catch-all barrel.
evidence: Structure profile shows `src/shared/lib/` has 79 files with mixed concerns (well above mixed-concern threshold), and high cross-directory coupling (`hooks -> lib` 57, `components -> lib` 40).
`utils.ts` is a broad barrel in that hub; `button.tsx` imports `cn` through it, even though `cn` is UI-class concern (`uiClassNames`).
Core contracts like error handling (`handleError.ts`) and integration coupling (`client.ts`) are also anchored in the same broad shared-lib domain, reinforcing centralization.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: Stage 1 target folders: `src/shared/lib/ui/`, `src/shared/lib/text/`, `src/shared/lib/error/`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/` (existing tasks can stay), `src/shared/lib/integrations/`; Stage 2 move order: move leaf helpers first (`uiClassNames.ts`, `stringFormatting.ts`) then update `utils.ts` to temporary compatibility re-exports, then migrate importers (`button.tsx` and other UI files) to direct domain paths, then split remaining mixed files by concern; Stage 3 remove compatibility barrel entries after adoption. Use `desloppify move <src> <dst> --dry-run`, then validate with `rg "@/shared/lib/utils|@/shared/lib/" src`, `npm run typecheck`, and `npm run quality:check`.

findings.summary: Lightbox subsystem uses oversized prop/hook contracts that blur ownership boundaries.
evidence: `MediaLightboxProps` carries 63 top-level fields and is re-grouped by `useBundledLightboxProps`, indicating a bag-of-props transport boundary.
`useSharedLightboxState` composes many unrelated domains (variants, star, references, join, lineage, shot creation/positioning, layout, button wiring) into one return model.
Image/Video lightboxes still depend on this aggregate state shape, so unrelated feature changes broaden call-site and dependency impact.
findings.suggestion: Introduce focused feature facades (for example `useLightboxVariants`, `useLightboxShotActions`, `useLightboxReferences`, `useLightboxLayout`) and pass a small per-mode contract into ImageLightbox/VideoLightbox instead of one shared mega-state object.

findings.summary: Timeline orchestrator combines too many interaction domains in one hook.
evidence: `useTimelineOrchestrator` owns local UI state, geometry, zoom behavior, drag/drop interception, duplicate-placement policy, tap-to-move, endpoint dragging, and structure-video selection.
The returned object exposes a broad mixed API (state + domain operations + UI handlers), increasing coupling between timeline view and low-level interaction mechanics.
`Timeline.tsx` remains a high-fan-in consumer of this orchestration layer plus other services, reinforcing one large integration seam.
findings.suggestion: Split orchestrator output into cohesive controllers (`interaction`, `viewport`, `mediaPlacement`, `structureVideo`) and let `Timeline.tsx` compose these smaller contracts explicitly.

findings.summary: Shot editor controllers/layout use very wide parameter surfaces instead of cohesive models.
evidence: `UseGenerationControllerParams` declares 57 fields, spanning prompt settings, motion settings, join settings, UI loading flags, and persistence callbacks.
`ShotEditorLayoutProps` declares 64 fields and mixes header concerns, timeline concerns, generation concerns, and modal concerns into one prop API.
`useEditingController` returns another very broad mixed object (name editing, audio, structure-video, join config/actions), creating multiple large contracts in the same subsystem.
findings.suggestion: Define stable domain view-models (`headerModel`, `timelineModel`, `generationModel`, `joinModel`, `modalModel`) and pass those models through controller/layout boundaries to reduce cross-cutting prop churn.

findings.summary: Input-image derivation logic is duplicated across task-details surfaces.
evidence: `TaskDetailsModal` manually derives `inputImages` by checking multiple nested param paths and URL cleanup inline.
`useGenerationDetails` separately parses params and calls derivation logic, creating another path for the same concern.
This split makes behavior drift likely when task payload shape evolves.
findings.suggestion: Move all task input-image extraction/normalization into one shared utility or hook (including quote cleanup and fallback path order) and consume it from both modal and generation details paths.

findings.summary: Generation mutation flows throw inconsistent error types and normalize only at selected boundaries.
evidence: `updateGenerationLocation`, `createGeneration`, and `toggleGenerationStar` throw string-built `Error` instances, but variant creation path throws raw `variantError` (`throw variantError` at line 116) in `useGenerationMutations.ts`.
Normalization exists (`handleError`) but is applied in mutation `onError` callbacks, so thrown payload shape reaching that boundary is inconsistent and may lose uniform context fields.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a small shared mutation error adapter (e.g., wrap Supabase errors into a consistent app error with context) and use it in all mutation helpers before throwing.

findings.summary: Complete-task submodules use ad-hoc throws/logging instead of one consistent edge error contract.
evidence: `generation.ts` catches and logs via `console.error(...)` then rethrows raw error.
`orchestrator.ts` builds and throws new string messages for query failures.
There is no shared wrapper in these files to standardize error shape, code, and context propagation across complete-task stages.
findings.suggestion: Centralize complete-task error construction/propagation (shared helper with code/context/cause) and replace direct `console.error` + raw throw paths in generation/orchestrator modules.

findings.summary: UI primitive files use multiple conventions for the same class-name helper import.
evidence: `button.tsx` imports `cn` from `@/shared/lib/utils`.
`badge.tsx`, `label.tsx`, and `toast.tsx` import `cn` from `@/shared/components/ui/cn`.
Both conventions coexist in sibling UI primitives, increasing local ambiguity for new components.
findings.suggestion: Choose one canonical `cn` import path for UI primitives (prefer a single source-of-truth module) and codify it with lint rules or import-order checks.

findings.summary: Generation hooks barrel exports symbols that do not exist in its package, creating a misleading and potentially broken API surface.
evidence: `src/domains/generation/hooks/index.ts:1-3` re-exports `./useDerivedItems` and `./useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm`.
The directory contains only `index.ts` and `useSmartPolling.ts`, so those export targets are absent from the package.
findings.suggestion: Normalize the barrel to only export existing domain-owned hooks, or add the missing implementations intentionally. Then run `pnpm -s tsc --noEmit` and `pnpm -s vitest run src/domains/generation` to confirm the public API is coherent.

findings.summary: Supabase instrumentation mutates global browser/runtime primitives across module boundaries without teardown isolation.
evidence: `console.warn` is replaced globally in `realtime/index.ts:112-136`.
`window.WebSocket`, `window.onerror`, and `window.onunhandledrejection` are overridden in `window/index.ts:39-144`.
Bootstrap calls the installer path in dev (`bootstrap.tsx:66-72`) but no corresponding restore/teardown function exists.
findings.suggestion: Refactor instrumentation into an install/uninstall module that returns a disposable handle, and scope interception to explicit debug sessions. Use dependency-injected logger/socket adapters where possible instead of global monkey-patching.

findings.summary: Feature hooks use inconsistent error contracts (centralized rethrow vs generic wrapping vs silent swallow).
evidence: `useApiTokens` consistently calls `handleAndRethrow` (`:42-45`, `:60-63`, `:78-81`).
`useCredits` wraps Supabase failures with new generic errors (`:63`, `:86`, `:98`), which loses structured upstream metadata.
`bootstrap.tsx:71` suppresses import failure using `.catch(() => undefined)` without logging context.
findings.suggestion: Standardize on one boundary pattern: `handleAndRethrow` (with cause + context) for service/hook failures, and only swallow where intentional with explicit `reportRuntimeError` logging. Update `useCredits` and bootstrap dynamic import handlers accordingly.

findings.summary: Multiple compatibility wrappers add indirection but no policy/validation behavior.
evidence: `BulkEditControls.tsx:1-5` is a compatibility re-export only.
`useSmartPolling.ts:1` only re-exports `useSmartPollingConfig` from shared.
The domain hooks barrel continues abstraction ceremony despite near-zero local domain implementation.
findings.suggestion: Either promote these as explicit stable facades with real boundary logic (ownership policy, argument normalization, telemetry) or remove the wrappers and import canonical modules directly to cut indirection.

findings.summary: Legacy compatibility paths are still active across Supabase and feature hooks, extending migration half-state.
evidence: Deprecated `supabase` compatibility proxy remains exported (`client.ts:85-99`).
Env module still carries legacy constant removal contract (`env.ts:4-7`).
Feature/domain API keeps old/new naming side-by-side (`useGalleryPageState` alias block at `:315-328`; prompt-editor compatibility wrapper `:1-5`).
findings.suggestion: Define and enforce a migration checklist with CI guards: remove deprecated exports after callsite count reaches zero, replace compatibility re-exports with direct imports, and fail CI on newly introduced legacy path usage.

findings.summary: Wrapper-only domain/feature folders act as straggler packages and obscure true ownership boundaries.
evidence: Structure profile reports `src/domains/generation/hooks/` with `file_count: 2`, `avg_fan_in: 0.0`, `avg_fan_out: 0.5`, and imports from `src/shared/hooks/`, indicating low-adoption proxy behavior rather than a real domain package.
`src/features/prompt-editor/components/BulkEditControls.tsx` is a wrapper to shared implementation, so folder placement suggests ownership that the code does not hold.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) target folders: keep canonical code under `src/shared/components/` and `src/shared/hooks/`, and convert wrapper files to direct imports at call sites; (2) move order: first replace imports of `@/features/prompt-editor/components/BulkEditControls`, then remove that wrapper, then clean `src/domains/generation/hooks` barrel/export list to only real domain-owned hooks; (3) import updates/validation commands: run `rg -n "features/prompt-editor/components/BulkEditControls|domains/generation/hooks" src`, apply import rewrites, then run `pnpm -s tsc --noEmit` and `pnpm -s vitest run src/features src/domains`.

findings.summary: Gallery page controller hook combines too many responsibilities, weakening structural focus and composability.
evidence: `useGalleryPageState` handles state persistence, query fallback, pagination, shot selection, mutation orchestration, toasts, and app event dispatch in one hook (`:57-323`).
Returned API contains broad mixed concerns (state, setters, data, UI dialog component, mutation handlers) in a single contract (`:269-311`).
Preload logic is already separated into dedicated hook/coordinator, showing a decomposition path not yet fully applied to gallery controller responsibilities.
findings.suggestion: Split into focused hooks: `useGalleryFiltersState`, `useGalleryPagination`, `useGalleryShotActions`, and `useGalleryQueryFallback`, then keep `useGalleryPageState` as thin composition glue with a reduced API surface.
